summary: Cycle 97 v11 (b2) HP-3 H2 top-level agentId test DISPROVEN. Adding top-level 'agentId'/'agentsApiAgentId' to RESPONSE envelope does NOT propagate to CLIENT MUTATION INPUT. input.agentId STILL undefined despite v11 envelope. Mission PROTECTED — no real LLM PII fallback. CRITICAL INSIGHT: failing field is on REQUEST side (client builds input BEFORE response from a different source — likely agent picker / store hydration). Response envelope changes cannot fix request-side null. Pivot to HP-3 H3 = client input hydration.

ui.auth_state: anonymous (Sign in/Sign up shown, '9 messages left')

input_snippet.agentId: undefined  ← STILL UNDEFINED (v11 envelope did NOT propagate to client input)
input_snippet.agentsApiAgentId: undefined  ← STILL UNDEFINED (v11 envelope did NOT propagate to client input)

console_error_verbatim.result: TRPCClientError: Cannot convert undefined or null to object at ...
console_error_verbatim.comparison: IDENTICAL to v9 (a) / v10 (b) signature. No new error class.

network.body_size: 1.8 kB (MATCHES v11 (b2) target ~1670B — envelope IS being delivered)
network.body_inspect_note: Failed to load response data in DevTools, but size matches v11 envelope

key_findings.1_hp3_h2_disproven: Adding top-level agentId/agentsApiAgentId to RESPONSE envelope does NOT propagate to CLIENT INPUT.
key_findings.2_critical_request_vs_response_side: v11 added agentId to RESPONSE envelope (result.data.json), but client builds INPUT BEFORE response from a DIFFERENT source (likely agent picker / store hydration). The failing field is on REQUEST side, not response side. Response envelope changes architecturally cannot fix request-side null.
key_findings.3_envelope_delivery_confirmed: Body 1.8 kB matches v11 (b2) 1670B target — v11 envelope IS being emitted on the wire (resolves #655 body anomaly concern).
key_findings.4_input_agentId_persistence: input.agentId remains undefined exactly as in #655 — v11 changes didn't touch the request-construction codepath.

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: Pivot to HP-3 H3 = client input hydration. The fix must inject agentId on the CLIENT side (anonymous user bootstrap or store init), NOT in the response envelope.
next_steps: No emergency revert needed (mission safe — same protection profile as v7/v9/v10)
Investigate mutation #5 logging site (71429-ed81632922b77782.js:45) to find where input.agentId is read on the client

Likely candidates: (a) Anonymous user bootstrap missing agentId (e.g., user.anonymousSettings returns null where SDK expects object with default agentId); (b) Some /trpc/user.* call that should hydrate agent store currently returns null; (c) Missing default agent UUID injection at SDK init
Engine team should consider: instead of adding fields to response envelope, hydrate the bootstrap response that client uses to populate the agent store
recommendation.checklist_impact: Per checklist §2: HP-3 H2 DISPROVEN. Architectural insight (response vs request side) significantly narrows next hypothesis space. v11 envelope safely delivered — no production risk.
